title: mentha hirsuta subsp. pyramidalis
intro: Mentha hirsuta subsp. pyramidalis, commonly known as woolly mint or hairy mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha hirsuta plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of woolly mint include its ability to alleviate nausea, improve digestion, and soothe sore throats, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Woolly mint can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
